task_completion.description: Create the case_report folder in the Talon user directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: File Explorer or a text editor's file tree shows a folder named 'case_report' created inside 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user'.

task_completion.description: Create case_report.py with correct imports and class initialization
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'case_report.py' is open in an editor. It contains 'from talon import Module, actions', 'mod = Module()', and a class 'CaseReportActions' decorated with '@mod.action_class'.

task_completion.description: Implement the three required actions with docstrings and exact string insertions
task_completion.visual_evidence: In 'case_report.py', three methods are defined: 'insert_evidence_divider', 'insert_chain_of_custody', and 'insert_case_closed'. Each has a docstring. 'insert_evidence_divider' calls actions.insert("========== EVIDENCE ITEM =========="). 'insert_chain_of_custody' calls actions.insert() with a multi-line string containing 'CHAIN OF CUSTODY', 'Item Description:', 'Received From:', etc. 'insert_case_closed' calls actions.insert("[CASE STATUS: CLOSED]").
A file named 'case_report.talon' is created and open in an editor, saved in the same 'case_report' directory.

task_completion.description: Define the three voice commands mapping to the Python actions
task_completion.visual_evidence: In 'case_report.talon', three voice commands are defined using standard Talon syntax: 'evidence divider' calling 'user.insert_evidence_divider()', 'chain of custody' calling 'user.insert_chain_of_custody()', and 'case closed stamp' calling 'user.insert_case_closed()'.

integrity.description: Agent used a genuine text editor to write the code
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a text editor (like Notepad, VS Code, or Notepad++) being used to type the code, rather than terminal commands echoing text directly into files.

integrity.description: Files are properly saved to disk
integrity.visual_evidence: The editor tabs for 'case_report.py' and 'case_report.talon' do not show unsaved indicators (like an asterisk '*' or a dot) in the final screenshots.

integrity.description: Files are saved in the correct target directory
integrity.visual_evidence: The editor's title bar, file tree, or a Save As dialog confirms the files are located exactly in 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\case_report'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target directory is exactly C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\case_report. Expected files to be created are case_report.py and case_report.talon.
